##RRUFFID=R250063
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=[(H_2_O)K]Mn_2_(Ti_2_Fe^3+^)(PO_4_)_4_O_2_(H_2_O)_10_·4H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Hagendorf-Süd pegmatite, Oberpfalz, Bavaria, Germany
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Isolated and intergrown pale-yellow, diamond-shaped tablets in association with fluorapatite, rockbridgeite, columbite and sub-micrometre rods of uranophane. Type sample, described in Grey et al (2023), with a revised structure and symmetry from Rewitzer et al (2024)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250063
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=[(H_2_O)_1.00_K_1.00_](Mn^2+^_21.51_Fe^2+^_0.49_)(Ti^4+^_1.08_Fe^3+^_0.73_Al_0.15_)(Ti^4+^_0.54_Fe^3+^_0.46_)(PO_4_)_4.00_[O_1.50_F_0.23_(OH)_0.27_](H_2_O)_10_·4H_2_O
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, profile is calculated using data collected at T = 100 K from Rewitzer et al (2024)
